statusLine: EU fuel stocks remain under pressure with 15 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, despite overall averages above the 90-day minimum.
fullAnalysis:
The EU's fuel security landscape shows concerning fragmentation despite headline numbers suggesting adequate reserves. While EU-wide averages for petrol (110.1 days), diesel (100.6 days), and jet fuel (95.1 days) all exceed the mandatory 90-day minimum, this masks severe shortfalls across member states. Romania faces the most acute situation with all three fuel types in critical status, including dangerously low petrol reserves at just 33.2 days.

Jet fuel emerges as the most vulnerable sector, with 17 of 27 countries holding critical or warning-level reserves. Poland's jet fuel stocks at 25.3 days and Portugal's at 26.7 days represent particularly severe shortfalls. This widespread jet fuel vulnerability reflects the sector's complex supply chains and the impact of recent Middle East disruptions on aviation fuel distribution.

The dramatic 42.9% surge in Brent crude prices since late February, now at $103.55 per barrel, compounds these supply pressures. This oil price shock has translated into significant consumer fuel costs, with Netherlands petrol reaching €2.389 per liter. The combination of tight physical stocks and elevated prices suggests the EU fuel market remains under significant strain from ongoing geopolitical supply disruptions.
keyPoints: 15 of 27 EU countries have critical fuel reserves in at least one category, with Romania critically short across all fuel types
Jet fuel shows the broadest vulnerability with 17 countries below safe levels, including Poland at just 25.3 days
Brent crude has surged 42.9% since late February to $103.55/barrel, driving consumer petrol prices above €2.30/liter in Netherlands and Denmark
Despite EU averages above 90-day minimums, the uneven distribution of reserves creates significant supply security risks across member states
Germany, the EU's largest economy, holds critical diesel reserves at 64.3 days, highlighting the widespread nature of current supply pressures
